description_long: This is a large, rectangular steel shipping container, commonly used for freight and storage in urban and industrial environments. The exterior is painted a weathered blue, with visible rust patches and signs of wear, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. Corrugated metal panels reinforce the sides and top, providing structural strength and rigidity. One end features double doors with locking bars, hinges, and printed white alphanumeric markings, typical of standardized shipping containers. The opposite sides are mostly unmarked, except for faded stenciling and additional rust streaks. The container sits directly on the ground, with no visible wheels or base supports.
description: A weathered blue steel shipping container with corrugated sides, rust patches, and double locking doors on one end.
description_view_0: Front-right view showing the double doors with locking bars, hinges, and white printed markings.
description_view_1: Back-left view displaying the long corrugated side and the plain, unmarked rear panel.
description_view_2: Rear-right view highlighting the unmarked corrugated side and the solid, closed rear panel.
description_view_3: Left-side view showing faded white stenciling ('PMC') on the corrugated metal surface.
attribute_labeling_or_signage: white printed markings and faded stenciling on doors and side panels
attribute_handles_or_grips: integrated locking bars and door handles on front doors
